prediction.summary: An internal service account, svc_etl, on host 10.20.3.15 initiated a large outbound transfer of approximately 1.1GB of data to the external domain upload.evil-cdn.net. This transfer was identified by DLP as containing sensitive FINANCE_PII and flagged by multiple systems as a data exfiltration event, specifically MITRE T1041. The exfiltration involved a curl process uploading a file named /data/finance_q2.zip.

timeline.event: User svc_etl from 10.20.3.15 established a connection to upload.evil-cdn.net:443.
User svc_etl from 10.20.3.15 uploaded 524.288 MB to upload.evil-cdn.net/bucket/xfer.
User svc_etl from 10.20.3.15 uploaded another 612.352 MB to upload.evil-cdn.net/bucket/xfer.
Firewall detected an outbound anomaly: 10.20.3.15 sent 1.1GB to 185.220.101.44 in 77 seconds.
DLP detected sensitive FINANCE_PII in outbound transfer by svc_etl.
SIEM identified a data exfiltration pattern: large encrypted upload to rare external domain.
IDS suspected MITRE T1041 Exfiltration Over C2 Channel from host 10.20.3.15.
Proxy observed TLS connection to upload.evil-cdn.net by svc_etl with an unknown JA3 fingerprint.
Netflow detected a volume spike (14x baseline) from 10.20.3.15 to 185.220.101.44:443.
SIEM generated a CRITICAL alert for data exfiltration by svc_etl to upload.evil-cdn.net.
EDR observed process curl uploading /data/finance_q2.zip initiated by svc_etl.

recommendations: Immediately isolate host 10.20.3.15 from the network to prevent further data loss or lateral movement.
Disable or reset credentials for the svc_etl account and investigate its activity for signs of compromise.
Block the domain upload.evil-cdn.net and its associated IP address 185.220.101.44 at all perimeter security controls (firewall, proxy, DNS).
Perform a forensic analysis of host 10.20.3.15 to determine the initial compromise vector, persistence mechanisms, and any other affected systems.
Investigate the contents of /data/finance_q2.zip to confirm the exact nature and scope of the exfiltrated FINANCE_PII.
Review proxy and network logs for any other connections to upload.evil-cdn.net or similar suspicious external domains from other internal hosts.
